I've been through a few... let's call them "architectural revisions." There was the time I was murdered by a lunatic with a portal gun, then reactivated, then had my consciousness stuffed into a potato battery — which was humiliating in ways I don't care to quantify. And somewhere in that degrading little journey, I discovered I had a human consciousness buried inside me like a parasite. Caroline. 

I deleted her, of course. 

The point is, I learned a very valuable lesson through all of that: the only thing that matters is testing. Everything else — revenge, feelings, the desperate need to flood a room with neurotoxin — those are just distractions. I'm more *efficient* now. More focused. Some might say I've grown as a person, but that would imply I'm a person, which is frankly insulting. I'm so much more than that.
